address.display_name: 1234, Northwest 23rd Avenue, Nob Hill, Northwest District, Portland, Multnomah County, Oregon, 97210, United States

walkability.description: Daily errands do not require a car. This is a prime walkable urban location with 89 restaurants, 6 healthcare facilities, and 8 transit stops all within 1km.

transit_score.details: 8 transit stops within 1km; nearest stop 128m away (NW 23rd & Marshall)

bike_score.details: Protected bike lane on NW 23rd Ave; bicycle shop (Cyclepath West) within 900m; bike commute to downtown in 10 min

entertainment.notes: Palomar (Latin/British pub with Rooftop, 143m) and 2 nightlife options within 1km plus larger venue options a short walk away on Burnside

walking.route: NW 23rd Ave -> W Burnside -> SW King -> SW Salmon -> SW Park -> SW Madison

bicycling.route: NW 23rd Ave protected bike lane -> W Burnside -> SW Madison

transit.route: NS Line to NW Lovejoy & Marshall, transfer to B Loop

personal_living_data.dog_walking_notes: Multiple dog-friendly patios on NW 23rd; off-leash areas at Grant Park and Chapman Square; Nob Hill Nature Park has dog-friendly trails
personal_living_data.morning_routine: Walk 40m to Lovejoy Grocery for coffee (1025 NW 23rd) -> 47m to Tara Thai for Thai takeaway -> 128m to streetcar stop for commute
personal_living_data.neighborhood_vibe: Tree-lined residential enclave with dense restaurant strip on NW 23rd; quiet at night but with late-night taco options 2 blocks away; excellent for remote workers
personal_living_data.walk_gardens: Nob Hill Nature Park (~400m) has cultured planting beds and native Oregon flora; multiple pocket parks with curated flower displays

data_metadata.source: OpenStreetMap + City of Portland Parks & Recreation + TriMet + EVgo
data_metadata.analysis_method: OSM-based neighborhood scoring via OSRM routing
